title: Latency, Throughput, Memory, Caching, Load, and Cost · Guided Program Studio
summary: A retrieval engineer and domain partner incrementally build RAG capacity and cost envelope, change one query, document, tenant, language, model, index, or policy condition, and keep earlier Python, provenance, accessibility, security, evaluation, and evidence invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of RAG capacity and cost envelope while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Allocate deadlines across admission, query processing, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, streaming, and cleanup with p50, p95, and p99 evidence.
Bound concurrent queries, embedding batches, index searches, reranks, model calls, queues, tenants, retries, and overload shedding.
Measure embedding, retrieval, reranking, context, prefix, and answer cache hit rate, saved work, staleness, authorization, invalidation, and quality.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Rag Stage Latency Budget
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop RAG engineering team handles case 3505 during a regulated document deletion request. Build deterministic Python evidence to budget stage latency, throughput, memory, provider work, cache validity, and cost without hiding quality loss; keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence; then insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

steps.title: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Rag Load Fault Capacity Test
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-load-fault-capacity-test-eosjts" that uses case 4101 to budget stage latency, throughput, memory, provider work, cache validity, and cost without hiding quality loss. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Rag Load Fault Capacity Test.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rag Load Fault Capacity Test into observable performance evidence. For RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Rag Load Fault Capacity Test, keep Rag Load Fault Capacity Test connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-load-fault-capacity-test-eosjts
# Competency: rag-load-fault-capacity-test.
# Case 4101: an enterprise retrieval migration canary.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice.
def evidence_rag_load_fault_capacity_test_eosjts(queries_per_second: int = 24, retrieval_ms: int = 45, rerank_ms: int = 38, generation_ms: int = 420, deadline_ms: int = 650, cost_microunits: int = 950, cache_hit: bool = False) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_eosjts = "eosjts-stjsoe-4101"
    assert evidence_variant_eosjts
    evidence_axes_eosjts = (
        "corpuseosjts",
        "querystjsoe",
        "tenant4101",
        "indexeosjts4101",
        "promptstjsoe4101",
        "evaluation4101eosjts",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_eosjts)) == 6
    if queries_per_second <= 0 or min(retrieval_ms, rerank_ms, generation_ms) < 0:
        return False, 0, "capacity-input"
    total_ms = retrieval_ms + rerank_ms + generation_ms
    if total_ms > deadline_ms:
        return False, total_ms, "latency-budget"
    if cost_microunits < 0:
        return False, cost_microunits, "cost-accounting"
    adjusted_cost = cost_microunits // 2 if cache_hit else cost_microunits
    assert adjusted_cost <= cost_microunits
    return True, adjusted_cost, "capacity-envelope"
